does: This work presents Mirakl's approach to integrating AI agents and enterprise-grade language models into their commerce platform, focusing on practical improvements to operational efficiency. The company demonstrates deployment of these agents to accelerate documentation processes, enhance customer support capabilities through intelligent response systems, and introduces Mirakl Nexus as an architecture designed to enable commerce workflows native to agentic interaction patterns. The core contribution centers on showing how large language models can be embedded into existing marketplace infrastructure to reduce friction in both internal operations and customer-facing services while building toward a future where agents themselves become primary actors in commercial transactions.
connects: This work builds on established research in conversational AI and large language models applied to business processes, extending previous work on AI-powered customer service systems and intelligent documentation generation. It connects to broader trends in enterprise AI adoption where companies integrate foundation models into workflow automation, similar to patterns in knowledge management and customer relationship management systems. The focus on agent-native design principles relates to emerging research on multi-agent systems and autonomous agents as first-class participants in software architecture, rather than merely tools for human operators. Mirakl's approach combines these threads by applying them specifically to the commerce and marketplace domain.
enables: This vision points toward a future where commerce platforms are fundamentally redesigned around agent capabilities rather than human-centric interfaces. It enables new possibilities for autonomous negotiation, real-time inventory management, and frictionless transaction execution between agents representing different stakeholders. The work opens directions for studying how trust, verification, and settlement mechanisms must evolve when agents conduct commerce at scale. It also suggests opportunities for developing richer agent communication protocols, standardized commerce ontologies that agents can reason about, and novel marketplace designs where agent efficiency and human oversight coexist. Such infrastructure could accelerate adoption of autonomous trading systems and agent-mediated supply chains.
